rulings.Case Title: QUINTANA VS A.P. GREEN INDUSTRIES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. BURDEN SHIFTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT CREATED BY DECLARATION OF MCTOSH BECAUSE NO IDENTIFICATION OF C&H EMPLOYEE. (AD(EX)
DENY FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN. NO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. (PB)
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED BURDEN SHIFTED BY SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. (PB)
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN. NO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. (PB)
GRANT, NO OPPOSITION FILED.
GRANT, NO OPPOSITION FILED.

RE METALCLAD - GRANT Metalclad Motion for Summary Judgment. Special Interrogatory responses introduced by plaintiff shift burden. See Exhibit A to Lovko Declaration. (CW) RE M. SLAYEN - DENY M. Slayen Motion for Summary Judgment. Defendant failed to shift burden. Standard discovery responses insufficient to shift burden because not focused on eliciting missing information specific to M. Slayen. See Schieding v Dinwiddie Construction Company (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64. Plaintiff's deposition testimony not sufficient to shift burden because Defendant failed to establish Plaintiff would have personal knowledge of decedent's working conditions. See Villa v. McFerren (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 733, 749. Affirmative evidence in Reply not sufficient. Facts not set forth is separate statement do not exist. See North Coast Business Park v Nielsen Construction Co (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 22. (CW)
GRANT, NO OPPOSITION FILED.
MOTION GRANTED. BURDEN SHIFTED BY DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES RE HAMILTON. (JH)
